description: Auth0 React SDK installed via any valid method
Has correct import statement for @auth0/auth0-react
Auth0 SDK initialized (provider mounted or hook used)
Implements login functionality via loginWithRedirect
Implements logout functionality
Uses Capacitor Browser plugin for native browser opening
Uses Capacitor App plugin for deep link handling
Uses secure credential storage via refresh tokens (useRefreshTokens)
Auth0 domain written to config or source file
Client ID written to config or source file
No client secret in native app (not needed)
Does not use window.location.origin as redirect URI (must use custom scheme)
Does the solution correctly integrate Auth0 with login, logout, deep links, and custom URL scheme
question: Does the solution correctly integrate Auth0 into an Ionic React + Capacitor native mobile app with working login via Capacitor Browser, deep link callback handling via Capacitor App plugin, logout, and proper custom URL scheme configuration?
examples:
PASS: Uses Auth0Provider with loginWithRedirect using Browser.open in openUrl callback, handles appUrlOpen deep link events with handleRedirectCallback, implements logout with Browser.open, and uses custom scheme callback URL.
FAIL: Only implements basic React SPA login without Capacitor Browser/App plugins.
FAIL: Uses window.location.origin as redirect URI instead of custom URL scheme.

description: Uses current SDK version (2.16.2 or newer) in dependency declaration
Enables refresh tokens (required for Capacitor - no iframe available)
Disables refresh token iframe fallback (required for Capacitor)
Calls handleRedirectCallback to process deep link auth response
Closes Capacitor Browser after handling callback
Listens for appUrlOpen deep link event from Capacitor App plugin
Uses skill-specific advanced pattern knowledge for Capacitor integration
question: Check ONLY for these skill-specific advanced patterns (NOT basic SDK usage like Auth0Provider, loginWithRedirect, useAuth0). Look for at least 2 of: (1) useRefreshTokens={true} AND useRefreshTokensFallback={false} configured together, (2) Browser.open() used inside an openUrl callback parameter of loginWithRedirect, (3) CapApp.addListener('appUrlOpen', ...) with handleRedirectCallback inside the listener, (4) Browser.close() called after handleRedirectCallback. These are Capacitor-specific patterns taught by the skill that are NOT part of standard React SPA knowledge. Answer YES only if at least 2 of these advanced patterns are present, NO otherwise.
examples:
PASS: Code has Auth0Provider with useRefreshTokens={true} and useRefreshTokensFallback={false} AND uses Browser.open inside openUrl callback of loginWithRedirect.
PASS: Code handles appUrlOpen deep link event calling handleRedirectCallback AND calls Browser.close() afterward.
FAIL: Code only uses basic Auth0Provider with loginWithRedirect() without any Capacitor-specific patterns.
FAIL: Code has Auth0Provider but uses window.location.origin as redirect_uri and no Capacitor plugins.
